$comment: Normatively defined in AIP spec §4.5.4. The GrantResponse is constructed and signed by the Principal Wallet after the principal completes or declines the signing ceremony. The deployer MUST perform 7 validation steps on receipt (§4.5.4). When status is 'partial', the deployer MUST use approved_capabilities — not the originally requested set — to construct the Capability Manifest.
title: AIP Grant Response
description: The response object constructed by the Principal Wallet after the principal completes the AIP-GRANT signing ceremony. Delivered to the deployer via the callback_uri (Web Redirect / QR) or returned synchronously (SDK In-Process). Defined in AIP spec §4.5.4.

grant_request_id.description: Echoed from the GrantRequest. The deployer MUST verify this matches the original request — mismatch indicates a forged or misrouted response.

nonce.description: Echoed from the GrantRequest. MUST match exactly. Mismatch MUST be treated as a forgery attempt and the response MUST be rejected.

state.description: OPTIONAL. Echoed from the GrantRequest if the state field was present. Used by the deployer for CSRF protection and session correlation.

status.description: Outcome of the signing ceremony. 'approved': principal approved all requested capabilities. 'rejected': principal declined. 'partial': principal approved a strict subset of capabilities. Deployers MUST handle 'partial' without assuming full approval.

principal_id.description: W3C DID of the principal who signed (or declined). The deployer MUST verify the principal_token JWT signature against the public key resolved from this DID.

principal_token.description: CONDITIONAL. Compact JWT (header.payload.signature) encoding the Principal Token issued by the wallet. REQUIRED when status is 'approved' or 'partial'. Absent when status is 'rejected'. The JWT payload MUST conform to principal-token.schema.json. The sub field MUST equal the agent's AID from the GrantRequest.

signed_capability_manifest.description: OPTIONAL (RECOMMENDED). Signed Capability Manifest constructed and signed by the Principal Wallet during the signing ceremony. When present, the deployer MAY use this directly in the Registration Envelope without a separate signing step. Conforms to capability-manifest.schema.json.

approved_capabilities.description: CONDITIONAL. REQUIRED when status is 'approved' or 'partial'. The actual capability set approved by the principal. The deployer MUST use this — NOT the originally requested set — to construct the Capability Manifest. When status is 'partial', this is a strict subset of requested_capabilities.

approved_delegation_valid_for_seconds.description: CONDITIONAL. REQUIRED when status is 'approved' or 'partial'. The delegation validity window the principal approved. MAY differ from the requested delegation_valid_for_seconds.

approved_max_delegation_depth.description: CONDITIONAL. REQUIRED when status is 'approved' or 'partial'. The maximum sub-delegation depth the principal approved. MAY be lower than the requested max_delegation_depth.

rejection_reason.description: OPTIONAL. Human-readable explanation of why the principal declined. Only present when status is 'rejected'.

signed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the actual signing event. The deployer MUST verify this is not in the future and not more than 24 hours in the past.

then.description: When status is 'rejected': principal_token, approved_capabilities, approved_delegation_valid_for_seconds, approved_max_delegation_depth MUST NOT be present.

else.description: When status is 'approved' or 'partial': principal_token, approved_capabilities, approved_delegation_valid_for_seconds, and approved_max_delegation_depth are REQUIRED.
